description: Auto-discover the recognised international consensus paper for a disease. Triggers when a new disease is added; promotes its top-ranked PubMed result into the official-guideline pointer block once a reviewer confirms.

nodes.label: Start
nodes.description: Entry point. Receives disease_slug and disease_name from the ticket description.

nodes.label: PubMed esearch
nodes.description: Query PubMed for consensus / best-practice reviews matching the disease name.

nodes.label: Parse PMIDs
nodes.description: Extract the PMID list from the esearch response.
nodes.python_source:
import json
body = json.loads(input.get('pubmed-esearch', {}).get('result', {}).get('body', '{}'))
pmids = body.get('esearchresult', {}).get('idlist', [])
return {'pmids': pmids}

nodes.label: PubMed esummary
nodes.description: Fetch title/authors/journal/year for every candidate PMID.

nodes.label: Format candidate list
nodes.description: Project each PMID's metadata into the {pmid, title, authors, journal, year} shape Gemma reads.

nodes.label: Rank with Gemma 4
nodes.description: Structured-output Gemma call: pick the recognised consensus document, return confidence + reasoning. Refuses to invent PMIDs that were not in the candidate list.
nodes.prompt: You are a clinical librarian. Pick the recognised international consensus / best-practice guideline for {{ context.start.result.disease_name }} from the candidate PubMed papers in {{ context.format-candidates.result.candidates_text }}. NEVER invent a PMID. Return best_pmid, title, authors, year, journal, confidence (0-1), reasoning.

nodes.label: Reject hallucinated PMIDs
nodes.description: Guard: refuse to persist a PMID Gemma did not see in the candidate list.

nodes.label: Persist pointer
nodes.description: Write the chosen paper to official_guideline_pointers with source='workflow'. A reviewer can promote it to source='reviewer' or replace it.
